response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile generalist engineer with deep craftsmanship habits (TDD, CI/CD, pairing) and genuine high-performance/low-latency experience, but their core stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. C++ does not even appear in their skills list and shows up only in dated roles (NI LabWindows/CVI C/C++ components and a university thesis), far below the hands-on C++/STL depth this role demands. There is no evidence of message brokers, MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA, IEC 62443/SDL secure engineering, or industrial automation. The gap against the role's central technical requirements is too large.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring
Proven high-performance/low-latency and concurrent systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching at scale)
Broad embedded/robotics and full-stack background with some C/C++ exposure
concerns: C++/STL is not a core skill — minimal and dated; absent from skills list
No industrial cybersecurity, IEC 62443/SDL, message brokers, or MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA experience
No industrial-automation or IoT-protocol domain background relevant to the role

key_factors.factor: Industrial security & protocols (IEC 62443, MQTT, message brokers)
